heading: Cluster Tier Auto-Scaling
body:
Atlas uses reactive and predictive auto-scaling for cluster tiers.
Atlas chooses its auto-scaling mechanism based on your cluster's type,
tier, and workload pattern.

- Reactive auto-scaling. Atlas uses thresholds, and not prediction,
  to trigger scaling events based on current resource usage. Reactive
  auto-scaling occurs **after** sustained high or low resource usage.
  To learn more, see [Reactive Auto-Scaling for Cluster Tier](cluster-autoscaling-reactive-overview).

- Predictive auto-scaling. Atlas uses machine learning to
  anticipate future scaling needs based on historical usage patterns
  and attempts to trigger scaling events **before** the forecasted workload
  spike arrives.

  Predictive auto-scaling is an extension of cluster tier auto-scaling and
  falls back to reactive auto-scaling. Atlas continues to rely on
  reactive auto-scaling to manage **unexpected** spikes in workload that
  aren't cyclical or predictable. Atlas uses predictive auto-scaling
  for [eligible for predictive auto-scaling](eligible-clusters-predictive-autoscaling).
  To learn more, see [predictive auto-scaling](predictive-autoscaling-overview).

> **Important:** If you create a cluster in Atlas and it's [eligible for reactive auto-scaling](eligible-clusters-reactive-autoscaling)
> and [eligible for predictive auto-scaling](eligible-clusters-predictive-autoscaling),
> both predictive and reactive auto-scaling mechanisms are enabled by
> default for the new cluster if you use the Atlas UI. Atlas then uses
> auto-scaling mechanisms based on your cluster's type, tier, and workload.
> If you use the Atlas Administration API, you must explicitly [enable auto-scaling](configure-cluster-autoscaling).
